_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse the effect of text structures, language features and literary devices such as extended metaphor, metonymy, allegory and symbolism on the interpretation of literary texts.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse the effect of text structures, language features and literary devices such as extended metaphor, metonymy, allegory and symbolism on the interpretation of literary texts.
y: These advanced literary devices create some of the richest and most enduring meanings in literature.
Understanding how these devices shape interpretation helps us engage with complex, canonical texts.
This analytical expertise is essential for senior literary study and enhances our appreciation of great writing.
eg: We analyse how an extended metaphor structures an entire text and shapes its interpretation.
We identify metonymy — where something closely associated represents the thing itself, like 'the Crown' for the monarchy.
We discuss how allegory creates a layer of meaning where characters and events represent abstract concepts.
We examine how symbolism creates resonance and depth in a literary text.
We compare how different literary devices create different types of interpretive challenges for readers.
We write a literary analysis that examines how specific devices shape the interpretation of a text.
